## SUMMARY

I run a personal agentic operating system, and I teach people how to do it. I have delivered live technical training and AI enablement to 200+ people (SVP through engineers) and public talks to 250+ live audiences, with a published set of five frameworks and working models, not slideware. I use Claude Code as core infrastructure: I pointed my system at its release notes, it surfaced three real bugs, I validated them, and it filed them upstream (#44707, #57946, #44711). I set up four specialist agents that designed and deployed a secure, zero-trust Azure environment in a completely autonomous 31-hour run, and I caught it when the AI documented the plan instead of the real change. I do not hand-type the code. I direct the agents, decide the architecture, and catch where they are wrong. Twenty-five years in infrastructure is how I know when AI output will not survive production.

## SELECTED PROJECT · BUILT AND PUBLISHED A PAID TECHNICAL TRAINING CURRICULUM (HANDS-ON LABS, ENROLLING)

I built and publish a paid technical training curriculum at awacs.ai/training: three courses on AI-orchestrated infrastructure, built around hands-on labs where students deploy dev, staging, and prod Azure environments, break things on purpose, and fix them with AI. I wrote the course content, the labs, and the free tools that ship with it (the Bicep Deployment Wizard, the Front9/Back9 naming demo). Course 1 is two days and enrolling now; Courses 2 and 3 follow. This is the work enablement actually is: turning a method into training people can run.

---

## SELECTED PROJECT · AI ENABLEMENT SESSIONS: 200+ ATTENDEES, SVP TO ENGINEERS

I delivered two AI enablement sessions to 200+ attendees (SVP through engineers) on AI-powered observability and specialist agent methodology, and translated agentic architecture into demos leadership could act on. First session: a live AI-powered observability demo showing LLM triage of 3,000+ backup jobs per week. Second session: specialist agent methodology, how to assign roles, structure handoffs, and constrain agent scope for production reliability. The goal was to close the gap between "AI is powerful" and "here is what to do Monday."

## UPSTREAM · ANTHROPICS/CLAUDE-CODE

My agentic system studied the Claude Code release notes, surfaced these issues, validated them under my direction, and filed them under my account:

- **#44707** Hook exit codes: exit(1) is silently non-blocking; only exit code 2 stops a PreToolUse operation. Undocumented; produces false confidence in enforcement architecture.
- **#57946** PreToolUse enforcement silently bypassed on Windows / Git Bash; hooks exit 49 with no output when python3 resolves to the Store stub. Labeled area:hooks area:security platform:windows.
- **#44711** Three CLI flags in --help missing from the docs (--brief, --file, --mcp-debug); confirmed in v2.1.92.

## TECHNICAL COMPETENCIES

**LLM & agentic systems:** Azure OpenAI, prompt engineering, orchestration (memory, state, tool-calling), MCP servers, sandbox tooling, high-fidelity internal demos, rapid iteration, player-coach technical leadership, agentic pipelines, LLM evaluation, shadow testing, application integration patterns
**Agent governance:** Claude Code PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks, exit(2) enforcement, CLAUDE.md governance layers (L0–L8), 5-question admission gate, Class A/B/C trust tiers, multi-agent orchestration (Analyzer + Librarian pattern), circuit breakers, SHA-256 brake events, append-only JSONL decision logs
**Enterprise deployment:** Azure Local (HCI), Azure Arc SSH, WDAC, Azure Functions, Key Vault, Managed Identity, Bicep, GraphQL API introspection, ServiceNow integration, enterprise auth patterns (cert-based SP, SAS rotation)
